isolation.notes: Used validator-specific user/org/scenario and dedicated hierarchy created only for this flow run.

assertionResults.reason: Dragging Director Alpha onto Director Beta opened subtree confirmation and, after confirming Move, the full subtree (Director Alpha -> Manager Alpha -> Engineer Alpha) moved under Director Beta.
Cycle attempt (dragging Director Beta into its own descendant path) and self-reparent attempt were both ignored; hierarchy remained unchanged and no additional move API call was triggered.

evidence.network: PATCH count for /api/employees/*/move remained 2 (no new move request from invalid drags)

assertionResults.reason: Three-level subtree move persisted with internal hierarchy intact: only Director Alpha managerId changed to Director Beta; Manager Alpha and Engineer Alpha manager chains stayed unchanged. State remained after refresh.
Drag-to-reparent in OrgChart moved Engineer Beta to a new manager, then Cmd+Z reverted it; Hierarchy view reflected the same reverted structure.

evidence.network: PATCH /api/employees/69e1d02c5a799536c8e08c0a/move observed for drag and again on undo
evidence.managerCheck: Engineer Beta managerId changed to 69e1d02b5a799536c8e08bfa after drag, then reverted to 69e1d02c5a799536c8e08c06 after Cmd+Z

frictions.description: agent-browser drag/get box with @ref tokens failed in this environment.
frictions.resolution: Used coordinate-based mouse drag with DOM-derived positions from agent-browser eval.

frictions.description: Native prompt-based org/scenario creation controls were not reliably automatable via direct button-click + prompt handling.
frictions.resolution: Created isolated org/scenario/hierarchy through authenticated in-session API calls via agent-browser eval, then validated all assigned assertions through UI interactions.
